steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: ”Kestrels' surroundings can significantly affect their hormone levels.” This means comparing studies of captive (lab) and wild settings should reveal different hormone levels.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer presents exactly this difference—luteinizing hormone levels are higher in captive than wild kestrels.

The other choices either deny the difference, describe individual variation rather than setting effects, or compare across species.

That is the correct answer.
